Title: Evolution of rhizobial siderophore utilization via accessory xeno-siderophore receptors and flexible intake machinery for self-produced siderophores

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TonB-dependent outer membrane siderophore receptor; canonical petrobactin receptor
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Iron-responsive; regulated by RirA as reported previously in this strain context
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In-frame deletion mutant; growth assay under iron limitation; cell-free supernatant rescue assays; competitive nodulation; experimental evolution background strain
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of fprA impairs utilization of self-produced petrobactin and causes growth defect under iron-limiting conditions; creates an altruistic siderophore-producing but uptake-defective strain
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Iron limitation induced by 150 μM 2,2'-bipyridine; neutral/alkaline pH; soybean nodulation with or without Fe3+ supplementation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Petrobactin-bound iron / iron acquired via petrobactin
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports bacterial iron acquisition, growth under iron limitation, and nodulation competitiveness
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fprA is the canonical receptor for the self-produced siderophore petrobactin in S. fredii. 2. Deletion of fprA creates a strain that still produces petrobactin but cannot efficiently use it, causing iron-limited growth defects. 3. The fprA mutant served as the key background for testing heterologous receptors and adaptive evolution of siderophore utilization machinery.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering or selecting strains with functional petrobactin uptake receptors should improve rhizobial persistence in iron-limited neutral/alkaline soils; receptor-defective strains illustrate public-goods behavior relevant to mixed inoculant design
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of receptor reduces fitness under iron limitation and may increase dependence on community context
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter in deletion background; heterologous receptor constructs used fprA promoter
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome resequencing of evolved derivatives (Illumina NovaSeq; accession NA)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a receptor-lacking background to test whether Bradyrhizobium receptors and adaptive mutations can restore utilization of self-produced siderophore

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In-frame deletion mutant; supernatant-based growth promotion assay of BD110 and SF45436ΔasbA
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion generates an altruistic rhizobactin-producing but uptake-defective strain whose supernatant promotes growth of xeno-siderophore exploiters
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports iron acquisition under iron limitation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. rhtA is required for rhizobactin uptake in S. meliloti. 2. The ΔrhtA mutant behaves as an altruistic siderophore producer. 3. Its supernatant can support growth of xeno-siderophore exploiters, though less effectively than S. fredii ΔfprA in this study.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for designing mixed communities or inoculants that modulate siderophore sharing/competition
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of uptake likely reduces self-fitness under iron limitation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as comparator strain rather than the main mechanistic focus

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Construction of double receptor mutant ΔfprAfoxA; soybean competitive nodulation assay
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of foxA together with fprA makes S. fredii more altruistic and increases competitor occupancy by xeno-siderophore exploiters during nodulation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soybean nodulation under low-nitrogen conditions with 300 or 0 μM Fe3+
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports iron acquisition and nodulation competitiveness
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. foxA is a characterized xeno-siderophore receptor in S. fredii. 2. Combined loss of fprA and foxA generates a more altruistic strain. 3. This receptor-deficient background increases nodulation success of xeno-siderophore exploiters under tested conditions.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Receptor content can be tuned to modulate competitiveness in mixed inoculants
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced receptor repertoire may reduce competitiveness under iron limitation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function discussed mainly in combination with fprA rather than alone

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Putative hemin receptor; TonB-dependent outer membrane β-barrel receptor
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Directly regulated by iron-responsive regulator RirA
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Selected during iron-limited evolution in ΔfprA backgrounds
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Experimental evolution; genome resequencing; reverse genetics introducing in-frame deletions; growth assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Adaptive in-frame deletions in the β-barrel domain partially restore growth of the fprA mutant under iron limitation, implicating c24120 in flexible petrobactin utilization machinery
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Petrobactin (inferred functional interaction after adaptation); TonB-ExbB-ExbD complex
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports bacterial iron acquisition and survival under iron limitation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Adaptive deletions in c24120 arose repeatedly in evolved ΔfprA backgrounds under iron limitation. 2. These mutations partially rescued growth defects, indicating functional rewiring of siderophore uptake. 3. c24120 likely contributes to utilization of self-produced petrobactin after structural modification.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for adaptive engineering of receptor flexibility in rhizobial inoculants for iron-poor soils
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered receptor specificity may affect heme uptake or other substrate preferences
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome resequencing of evolved clones (Illumina NovaSeq; accession NA)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally annotated as putative hemin receptor; direct petrobactin transport not proven in wild type but strongly supported by adaptive genetics

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ExbD; component of TonB-ExbB-ExbD energy transduction complex
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Selected during iron-limited evolution in ΔfprA::SR5 background
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Experimental evolution; genome resequencing; reverse genetics and complementation; growth assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A frameshift truncation in ExbD partially restores growth of receptor-deficient S. fredii under iron limitation, indicating altered energy coupling for siderophore uptake
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TonB; ExbB; outer membrane siderophore receptors
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports high-affinity iron transport via TonB-dependent receptors
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A frameshift mutation in exbD appeared in an evolved clone with restored iron-limited growth. 2. Reverse genetics showed this mutation partially rescues the ΔfprA growth defect. 3. Complementation with wild-type exbD partially reduced the rescue phenotype, confirming adaptive contribution.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suggests that tuning TonB-dependent energy-coupling modules may broaden siderophore-use capacity
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutations in energy transduction machinery may alter transport selectivity or membrane physiology
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome resequencing of evolved clones (Illumina NovaSeq; accession NA)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contribution may act together with introduced SR5 receptor and/or porin mutation

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Y158C porin mutation was associated with improved iron-limited growth in an evolved clone, suggesting a role in low-affinity or altered envelope permeability linked to siderophore utilization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A porin mutation was identified in an evolved clone with enhanced growth under iron limitation. 2. The mutation is located in the β-barrel domain. 3. The paper suggests porin-associated envelope changes may contribute to siderophore-dependent iron acquisition, although direct validation was limited.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving envelope transport properties in engineered rhizobia
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could affect general permeability and stress sensitivity
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome resequencing of evolved clones (Illumina NovaSeq; accession NA)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contribution inferred as part of evolved phenotype, possibly together with SR5 and exbD mutation

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Putative polysaccharide deacetylase; carbohydrate esterase 4 superfamily; NodB motif-containing
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Experimental evolution; genome resequencing; reverse genetics of W84G; combinatorial mutant analysis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: W84G mutation significantly enhances growth of the fprA mutant under iron limitation and acts additively with c24120 deletion, indicating cell-envelope remodeling contributes to siderophore utilization
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports iron acquisition and fitness under iron limitation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A W84G mutation in b56940 was identified in an evolved clone of ΔfprA. 2. This mutation alone significantly improved growth under iron limitation. 3. Combined with c24120 deletion it produced a phenotype close to the evolved clone, supporting a role in adaptive uptake machinery.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights envelope polysaccharide remodeling as an engineering route to improve siderophore use
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter cell surface properties and host or stress interactions
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome resequencing of evolved clones (Illumina NovaSeq; accession NA)
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a canonical siderophore gene but clearly part of the adaptive machinery restoring nutrient acquisition

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Catecholate-type TonB-dependent siderophore receptor; Fiu homolog
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Present in BD110 as one of eight siderophore receptors
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript detected after heterologous expression under iron limitation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in ΔfprA; RT-qPCR; experimental evolution; growth assays; complementation of adaptive mutation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes with adaptive mutation b56780-T91P to partial restoration of growth of S. fredii ΔfprA under iron limitation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Petrobactin-mediated iron uptake in engineered background
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves fitness under iron limitation when combined with adaptive mutations
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SR1 was expressed in S. fredii ΔfprA under iron limitation. 2. Evolved clones carrying SR1 gained improved growth. 3. Genetic evidence indicates SR1 contributes to rescue together with adaptive mutation in b56780.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate accessory receptor for broadening siderophore exploitation in inoculant strains
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Benefit appears context dependent and requires adaptive background
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR; genome resequencing of evolved clones
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Accessory xeno-siderophore receptor rather than native self-siderophore receptor

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Catecholate-type TonB-dependent siderophore receptor; FepA homolog
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript detected after heterologous expression under iron limitation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in ΔfprA; experimental evolution; resequencing; growth assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with evolved restoration of growth under iron limitation in combination with exbD and/or porin adaptive mutations
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Petrobactin-mediated iron uptake in engineered background
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enhances growth under iron limitation in engineered strains
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SR5-expressing ΔfprA strains yielded evolved clones with strong growth rescue. 2. Rescue involved adaptive mutations including exbD frameshift and porin mutation. 3. SR5 is inferred to contribute to petrobactin uptake flexibility in the evolved background.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory receptor for engineering broad iron-scavenging capacity
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Function may depend on envelope/energy-coupling mutations
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR; genome resequencing of evolved clones
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct petrobactin transport not biochemically shown

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Hydroxamate-type TonB-dependent siderophore receptor; FiuA homolog
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: One of eight siderophore receptors in BD110; essential for ferrichrome intake in BD110
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript detected after heterologous expression under iron limitation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in ΔfprA; experimental evolution; genome resequencing; reverse genetics of c24120 mutation; growth assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Together with adaptive c24120 mutation, SR6 contributes to improved growth of S. fredii ΔfprA under iron limitation and may mediate petrobactin uptake in evolved cells
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Petrobactin-mediated iron uptake in engineered background
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports iron acquisition and fitness under iron limitation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SR6-expressing ΔfprA strains generated evolved clones with enhanced growth. 2. Reverse genetics showed c24120 adaptive deletion partially rescues growth, and the evolved clone exceeds this rescue, indicating SR6 contribution. 3. The paper proposes SR6 may help uptake petrobactin after adaptive mutations.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising accessory receptor for engineering siderophore-use plasticity in rhizobia
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate spectrum may be altered in ways not fully resolved
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR; genome resequencing of evolved clones
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Known ferrichrome receptor in BD110; petrobactin role here is adaptive and indirect

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Hydroxamate-type TonB-dependent siderophore receptor; FiuA homolog
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript detected after heterologous expression under iron limitation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in ΔfprA; RT-qPCR; experimental evolution; growth assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Certain evolved clones carrying SR8 restored robust growth under iron limitation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Petrobactin-mediated iron uptake in engineered background
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SR8 was expressed in the ΔfprA background. 2. Evolved SR8-bearing clones showed wild-type-like growth under iron limitation. 3. Specific causal adaptive mutation details for SR8 rescue were not fully dissected in the text.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential accessory receptor for broadening siderophore exploitation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mechanism of rescue remains incompletely defined
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because robust evolved rescue was reported

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Essential biosynthetic step required for petrobactin production; exact reaction not specified in this paper
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for formation of target siderophore
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key required step; essential for pathway function
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In-frame deletion mutant; siderophore production assay; cell-free supernatant assays; deletion in evolved clones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion abolishes petrobactin production and removes the growth-supporting effect of supernatant; deleting asbA in evolved clones collapses restored growth under iron limitation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates Fe3+ to support iron acquisition, growth under iron limitation, and competitive nodulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Iron limitation induced by 2,2'-bipyridine; pH 7 and pH 9; soybean nodulation conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. asbA is essential for petrobactin biosynthesis in S. fredii. 2. The ΔasbA mutant cannot provide siderophore-mediated growth support to exploiters. 3. Adaptive restoration of growth in evolved ΔfprA derivatives depends on intact asbA, demonstrating reliance on self-produced petrobactin.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential target to retain or enhance in inoculants adapted to neutral/alkaline iron-limited soils; can be combined with receptor engineering
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of asbA severely reduces fitness under iron limitation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also used to build engineered xeno-siderophore exploiter ΔasbA-GFP

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Essential step required for rhizobactin biosynthesis; exact reaction not specified in this paper
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for formation of target siderophore
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key required step; essential for pathway function
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In-frame deletion mutant; supernatant-based growth promotion assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion abolishes siderophore production-dependent growth support for BD110 and engineered exploiter strains
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates Fe3+ for iron acquisition under limitation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. rhbC is essential for rhizobactin biosynthesis in S. meliloti. 2. Loss of rhbC removes the positive effect of producer supernatant on xeno-siderophore exploiters. 3. This confirms that growth rescue by producer supernatant depends on siderophore biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for modulating public siderophore supply in synthetic inoculant consortia
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Comparator pathway gene rather than the central evolutionary focus

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: c24120; exbBD; siderophore-related functions including asbA context
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Siderophore biosynthesis and uptake homeostasis for iron acquisition
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Controls expression of siderophore uptake/iron transport genes; c24120 and exbBD are stated to be regulated by RirA
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of siderophore production and uptake under iron limitation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Use of rirA mutant as siderophore-overproducing control; prior characterization cited in this strain; comparative CAS assay context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of RirA leads to siderophore overproduction in S. fredii
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports iron acquisition and fitness under iron limitation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RirA functions as an iron-responsive regulator in S. fredii. 2. The rirA mutant was used as a siderophore-overproducing control. 3. RirA directly regulates c24120 and exbBD, linking regulatory control to adaptive siderophore utilization machinery.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever for tuning siderophore output in inoculant strains, though overproduction may alter community interactions
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overproduction of siderophores may impose metabolic burden or alter competition
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not the central experimental subject but directly tied to core siderophore phenotypes in the Results
